feed.title: Andy Zhu's blog
feed.subtitle: Notes on cryptography, algorithms, math, and other things I find interesting.

entry.title: The Discrete Fourier Transform
entry.summary: From continuous frequencies to roots of unity — how the DFT, FFT, and NTT turn O(n²) convolutions into O(n log n), with applications in competitive programming and the real world.

entry.title: The elliptic curve on my homepage, demystified
entry.summary: What the animated curve behind every page is actually computing — and why the same operation secures half the internet.

entry.title: FRI: fast low-degree testing by recursive folding
entry.summary: How a clever split of a polynomial into its even and odd parts lets a verifier check degree in logarithmic queries — the recursive trick behind modern STARKs.
